name: apple-mail-blade-mcp
title: Apple Mail
description: On-disk Apple Mail reader — Envelope Index SQLite + .emlx bodies + attachments. Read-only; never opens the network. NLTagger entity extraction under the deterministic-extraction carve-out (DD-240).
tagline: Tens of GB of personal correspondence, on-device, no network
author: groupthink-dev

tools.name: apple_mail_list_accounts
tools.description: List Mail accounts derived from mailboxes.url prefixes. No arguments.

tools.name: apple_mail_list_mailboxes
tools.description: List mailboxes with optional account_id filter pushed into SQL.

tools.name: apple_mail_list_messages
tools.description: List messages in a mailbox with server-side mailbox_id/since/until/limit/offset (index-only; never opens .emlx).

tools.name: apple_mail_search_messages
tools.description: FTS5 sidecar search with server-side query/account_id/mailbox_id/since/limit; LIKE fallback when FTS dormant.

tools.name: apple_mail_head
tools.description: Cheap metadata lookup by message_id (index-only; never opens .emlx).

tools.name: apple_mail_read_message
tools.description: Open .emlx for message_id, parse RFC822 + multipart MIME, return decoded headers + body (HTML optional).

tools.name: apple_mail_read_attachment
tools.description: Read attachment bytes by message_id + attachment_id from the on-disk Attachments subtree; base64-encoded with 25MB hard cap.

tools.name: apple_mail_read_thread
tools.description: Reconstruct a thread starting from message_id via conversation_id + In-Reply-To/References. Cross-account hops flagged.

tools.name: apple_mail_extract_entities
tools.description: Deterministic NLTagger + NSDataDetector entity extraction over the message body (DD-240 invariant #3 carve-out). No persistence; recomputes per call.

tools.name: apple_mail_index_status
tools.description: Return current MailIndexHealth snapshot — lag rows, FTS readiness, dormancy flag.

tools.name: apple_mail_reindex
tools.description: Post a reindex request via DistributedNotificationCenter; force=true bypasses the AC-power gate. Idempotent — internal state change only, no external side effects.

setup.blurb: Read on-disk Mail.app data — accounts, mailboxes, messages, threads, attachments, NLTagger entities. Tens of GB of personal correspondence the live IMAP/JMAP layer can't see (on-my-Mac mailboxes, removed-account archives, pre-migration history).
setup.help_text: Local-corpus class blade (DD-240). Library form factor — compiled into Stallari, not a separate subprocess. Requires Full Disk Access on the Stallari daemon binary so it can read ~/Library/Mail/V10/. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access and enable Stallari.app + the stallari-helper binary inside it.

help.label: Grant Full Disk Access
help.description: Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access. Add /Applications/Stallari.app and /Applications/Stallari.app/Contents/MacOS/stallari-helper, then toggle both on.

help.label: DD-241 — apple-mail-blade-mcp
help.description: Library form factor inside StallariKit (DD-240 invariant #8 — never advertised externally).

prerequisites: macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later — V10 schema only in v0.1.0
Full Disk Access granted to the Stallari daemon binary (stallari-helper) — needed to read ~/Library/Mail/V10/
Mail.app must have been launched at least once (so Envelope Index exists)

notes: 9 read-only tools (list_accounts, list_mailboxes, list_messages, search_messages, head, read_message, read_attachment, read_thread, extract_entities). Library form factor (DD-241 Phase A.5) — compiled into StallariKit via SPM dep on apple-mail-blade-mcp v0.1.0. **Never advertised on the daemon's :9847/mcp HTTP MCP surface per DD-240 invariant #8** — consumed only via the in-process Dispatcher path through AppleMailBladeWiring. testMailNotAdvertisedExternally regression guard locks this in stallari-harness. v0.1.0 hard caps: 25MB attachment, 100MB message, 32 multipart depth. NLTagger entity extraction (people/orgs/places/dates) under DD-240 invariant #3 deterministic-extraction carve-out — no LLM, no user-loaded CoreML.
